result.stdout:
Veld — contract-first, multi-stack API code generator.

Write .veld contracts once, generate typed frontend SDKs and backend
service interfaces for any framework. Zero runtime dependencies.

  veld init                    Scaffold a new project
  veld generate                Generate from veld.config.json
  veld generate --dry-run      Preview what would be generated
  veld watch                   Auto-regenerate on file changes
  veld validate                Check contracts for errors
  veld lint                    Analyse contract quality
  veld clean                   Remove generated output
  veld openapi                 Export OpenAPI 3.0 spec
  veld diff                    Show changes since last generation
  veld docs                    Generate API documentation
  veld fmt                     Format .veld contract files
  veld lsp                     Start the LSP server
  veld setup                   Auto-configure project imports
  veld doctor                  Diagnose project health
  veld completion              Generate shell completions

Backends:  node-ts, node-js, python, go, rust, java, csharp, php
Frontends: typescript, javascript, react, vue, angular, svelte, dart, kotlin, swift, types-only, none
Aliases:   node → node-ts, js/javascript → node-js, ts → typescript, react → react-hooks

Usage:
  veld [command]

Available Commands:
  ci          Generate code and configure project paths in one step (non-interactive)
  clean       Remove the generated output directory
  completion  Generate shell completion scripts
  diff        Show changes between current and freshly generated output
  docs        Generate API documentation from the contract
  export      Export contract to other formats (OpenAPI, GraphQL, SQL, docs)
  generate    Generate code from a .veld contract
  graphql     Export a GraphQL SDL schema from the contract
  help        Help about any command
  init        Scaffold a new Veld project in the current directory
  lint        Analyse the contract for quality issues
  lsp         Start the Veld LSP server (stdin/stdout)
  openapi     Export an OpenAPI 3.0 spec from the contract
  registry    Manage registry connections and packages
  schema      Generate a database schema from the contract
  setup       Auto-configure project files for seamless imports
  validate    Parse and validate a .veld contract file
  watch       Watch .veld files and auto-regenerate on change

Flags:
  -h, --help      help for veld
  -q, --quiet     suppress non-essential output
  -v, --verbose   enable verbose output
      --version   version for veld

Use "veld [command] --help" for more information about a command.

result.stdout:
Generate shell completion scripts for Veld.

  bash:       source <(veld completion bash)
  zsh:        veld completion zsh > "${fpath[1]}/_veld"
  fish:       veld completion fish | source
  powershell: veld completion powershell | Out-String | Invoke-Expression

Usage:
  veld completion [bash|zsh|fish|powershell] [flags]

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for completion

Global Flags:
  -q, --quiet     suppress non-essential output
  -v, --verbose   enable verbose output

result.stdout:
Generates to a temporary directory and compares file-by-file with the
existing output. Useful for CI: `veld diff --exit-code` fails if stale.

Usage:
  veld diff [flags]

Flags:
      --exit-code   exit with code 1 if changes detected (useful for CI)
  -h, --help        help for diff
      --stat        show summary only (files changed/added/removed)

Global Flags:
  -q, --quiet     suppress non-essential output
  -v, --verbose   enable verbose output

result.stdout:
Generate API documentation from the contract

Usage:
  veld docs [flags]

Examples:
  veld docs
  veld docs -o api-docs.html
  veld docs --format=markdown

Flags:
      --format string   output format (html, markdown) (default "html")
  -h, --help            help for docs
  -o, --output string   output file (default: docs.html or docs.md)

Global Flags:
  -q, --quiet     suppress non-essential output
  -v, --verbose   enable verbose output

result.stdout:
Reads .veld files and outputs canonically formatted versions.
Use --write to update files in place.

Usage:
  veld fmt [files...] [flags]

Examples:
  veld fmt
  veld fmt --write
  veld fmt veld/models/user.veld

Flags:
  -h, --help    help for fmt
  -w, --write   update files in place

Global Flags:
  -q, --quiet     suppress non-essential output
  -v, --verbose   enable verbose output

result.stdout:
Generates typed backend interfaces and a frontend SDK from your .veld contract.

Every file is (re)generated by default — deterministic and safe for CI/CD.
Pass --incremental to skip modules whose source files have not changed
(intended for local development, not production pipelines).

Backends: node-ts, node-js, python, go, rust, java, csharp, php,
          openapi, database, dockerfile, cicd, env, scaffold-tests
Frontends: typescript, javascript, react, vue, angular, svelte,
           dart, kotlin, swift, types-only, none
Aliases:   node → node-ts, js/javascript → node-js

Usage:
  veld generate [flags]

Examples:
  veld generate
  veld generate --backend=node --frontend=react
  veld generate --backend=go --frontend=vue
  veld generate --frontend=types-only
  veld generate --backend=openapi
  veld generate --backend=dockerfile
  veld generate --dry-run

Flags:
      --backend string              backend target (cicd, csharp, database, dockerfile, env, go, java, node-js, node-ts, openapi, php, python, rust, scaffold-tests)
      --backend-framework string    framework for the backend emitter (express, flask, chi, spring, axum, aspnet, laravel — default: plain/none)
      --dry-run                     preview what would be generated without writing files
      --force                       generate despite breaking changes without prompting (overrides interactive gate)
      --frontend string             frontend SDK (angular, dart, javascript, kotlin, react, svelte, swift, types-only, typescript, vue, none)
      --frontend-framework string   framework wrapper for the frontend SDK (react, vue, angular, svelte — default: none)
  -h, --help                        help for generate
      --incremental                 skip unchanged modules (dev only — not for production builds)
      --input string                input .veld file
      --out string                  output directory
      --server-sdk                  also emit a server-to-server typed client in generated/server-client/
      --setup                       auto-configure project files for seamless imports after generation
      --strict                      exit non-zero if any breaking changes are detected (ideal for CI/CD pipelines)
      --validate                    emit zero-dep runtime validators and wire into route handlers (overrides config)

Global Flags:
  -q, --quiet     suppress non-essential output
  -v, --verbose   enable verbose output

commands.payload:
Generates typed backend interfaces and a frontend SDK from your .veld contract.

Every file is (re)generated by default — deterministic and safe for CI/CD.
Pass --incremental to skip modules whose source files have not changed
(intended for local development, not production pipelines).

Backends: node-ts, node-js, python, go, rust, java, csharp, php,
          openapi, database, dockerfile, cicd, env, scaffold-tests
Frontends: typescript, javascript, react, vue, angular, svelte,
           dart, kotlin, swift, types-only, none
Aliases:   node → node-ts, js/javascript → node-js

Usage:
  veld generate [flags]

Examples:
  veld generate
  veld generate --backend=node --frontend=react
  veld generate --backend=go --frontend=vue
  veld generate --frontend=types-only
  veld generate --backend=openapi
  veld generate --backend=dockerfile
  veld generate --dry-run

Flags:
      --backend string              backend target (cicd, csharp, database, dockerfile, env, go, java, node-js, node-ts, openapi, php, python, rust, scaffold-tests)
      --backend-framework string    framework for the backend emitter (express, flask, chi, spring, axum, aspnet, laravel — default: plain/none)
      --dry-run                     preview what would be generated without writing files
      --force                       generate despite breaking changes without prompting (overrides interactive gate)
      --frontend string             frontend SDK (angular, dart, javascript, kotlin, react, svelte, swift, types-only, typescript, vue, none)
      --frontend-framework string   framework wrapper for the frontend SDK (react, vue, angular, svelte — default: none)
  -h, --help                        help for generate
      --incremental                 skip unchanged modules (dev only — not for production builds)
      --input string                input .veld file
      --out string                  output directory
      --server-sdk                  also emit a server-to-server typed client in generated/server-client/
      --setup                       auto-configure project files for seamless imports after generation
      --strict                      exit non-zero if any breaking changes are detected (ideal for CI/CD pipelines)
      --validate                    emit zero-dep runtime validators and wire into route handlers (overrides config)

Global Flags:
  -q, --quiet     suppress non-essential output
  -v, --verbose   enable verbose output

result.stdout:
Runs static analysis on your .veld contract and reports warnings and errors.
Unlike 'veld validate' (which checks structural correctness), 'veld lint'
flags patterns that are legal but likely unintentional — unused models,
empty modules, duplicate routes, missing descriptions, and more.

Exits 0 when no issues are found. Use --exit-code to fail on any finding.

Usage:
  veld lint [flags]

Examples:
  veld lint
  veld lint --exit-code

Flags:
      --exit-code   exit with a non-zero status if any issues (including warnings) are found
  -h, --help        help for lint

Global Flags:
  -q, --quiet     suppress non-essential output
  -v, --verbose   enable verbose output

result.stdout:
Patches config files (tsconfig.json, pubspec.yaml, go.mod, etc.) so that
generated code can be imported without manual edits.

Reads backend/frontend from veld.config.json and applies the appropriate patches.
If the generated output path has changed, existing entries are updated in place.

Use --backend-dir / --frontend-dir to point at project folders outside the
current directory, so you don't need a config file in each folder.

Usage:
  veld setup [flags]

Examples:
  veld setup
  veld setup --backend-dir=../server --frontend-dir=../client
  veld setup --out=./output --backend=go --frontend=react

Flags:
      --backend string        backend target override
      --backend-dir string    directory containing backend project files (default: current directory)
      --frontend string       frontend SDK override
      --frontend-dir string   directory containing frontend project files (default: current directory)
  -h, --help                  help for setup
      --input string          input .veld file
      --out string            output directory override

Global Flags:
  -q, --quiet     suppress non-essential output
  -v, --verbose   enable verbose output

result.stdout:
Watches all .veld files and the config for changes, then performs a full
regeneration of all outputs. This ensures shared artifacts (types, barrels,
middleware, _internal.ts) are always consistent. Safe to run during development.

Usage:
  veld watch [flags]

Flags:
      --backend string    backend target (cicd, csharp, database, dockerfile, env, go, java, node-js, node-ts, openapi, php, python, rust, scaffold-tests)
      --frontend string   frontend SDK (angular, dart, javascript, kotlin, react, svelte, swift, types-only, typescript, vue, none)
  -h, --help              help for watch
      --input string      input .veld file
      --out string        output directory

Global Flags:
  -q, --quiet     suppress non-essential output
  -v, --verbose   enable verbose output
